title: Brown-led Celts hand Thunder first loss in month
content: Jaylen Brown scored 14 of his 31 points in a pivotal third quarter, and the Boston Celtics rallied to beat the Oklahoma City Thunder 119-109 on Wednesday night, snapping the defending champions' 12-game win streak.

title: Nacua sued over alleged antisemitic remark, bite
content: A woman in Los Angeles has filed a civil lawsuit against Puka Nacua alleging the Rams wide receiver made an "unprovoked antisemitic statement" and later bit her on the shoulder.

title: Fried's gem lifts Yanks despite Judge's 4-K opener
content: Max Fried allowed just two hits in 6 1/3 scoreless innings, and the Yankees cruised past the Giants 7-0 on Wednesday night despite Aaron Judge's first career hitless Opening Day.

content: Amid speculation he could be a candidate for the vacancy at North Carolina, Arizona coach Tommy Lloyd insisted all his attention is with his program ahead of Thursday's Sweet 16 game against Arkansas.

content: Ashok Choudhary, a close aide of Chief Minister Nitish Kumar, was selected for the post of Assistant Professor after he cleared the Bihar State University Service Commission recruitment exam 2020 in Political Science.

title: Office commute times nearly identical in Bengaluru, Hyderabad: report
content: Data from MoveInSync indicates that the average one-way commute is about 59 minutes in Bengaluru and 58 minutes in Hyderabad, suggesting similar overall travel burdens

title: 'Friendly nations' only: Iran allows India, Pakistan, 3 other countries to use Strait of Hormuz
content: Iran has opened the Strait of Hormuz for friendly nations like India, China, Russia, Iraq, and Pakistan. This comes as UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres urged an end to the conflict and the reopening of the vital waterway. The Strait of Hormuz is a crucial oil route, and its volatility impacts global energy flows amid regional tensions.

title: 'He'd rather die in prison': Imran Khan unbowed, sons share powerful message
content: Imran Khan, imprisoned for nearly three years, remains resolute, his sons reveal.  Despite health concerns and his wife's alleged mistreatment, Khan states authorities will never break him. He finds strength in meditation, viewing his incarceration as a trial.  His sons, awaiting visas, last spoke to him for 28 minutes, noting his unwavering principles.

title: Video: Bus falls off ferry into Padma River in Bangladesh; 23 dead
content: Tragedy struck Bangladesh as a passenger bus plunged into the Padma River, claiming at least 23 lives. Rescue efforts are ongoing to find missing individuals after the bus fell from a ferry near Daulatdia. Officials confirmed 23 bodies recovered, with some survivors reaching safety. The submerged bus was eventually lifted hours later.

title: Microsoft HR head makes it clear to employees it's time to change
content: Microsoft's Chief People Officer Amy Coleman announced a significant HR restructuring, prioritizing adaptability and speed for the AI era. This shakeup includes leadership changes and new teams focused on accelerating HR operations, employee experience, and workforce skilling. The move signals a shift towards agility and a faster pace for the tech giant.

title: BCCI warns IPL captains on catch rules, provides update on impact player rule
content: IPL captains have been warned about strict enforcement of fielder movement rules during catches, with officials emphasizing complete control before celebrations. The Impact Player rule will also remain for at least another season, despite concerns from players like Axar Patel about its impact on all-rounder development.

title: ‘Trump doesn’t bluff’: WH warns Iran, says ‘will unleash hell’ against 'any miscalculation'
content: The White House issued a stern warning to Iran, stating President Trump is prepared to "unleash hell" if Tehran rejects a deal to end the Middle East conflict. White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt asserted that Iran has been militarily defeated and will face intensified strikes if it fails to accept this reality.

title: ‘Not one, but two Sheesh Mahals’: CAG report dominates Delhi assembly business
content: Delhi Assembly proceedings were dominated by the CAG report on former CM Arvind Kejriwal's official residence renovation, which cost Rs 33.6 crore, 342% above estimate. Chief Minister Rekha Gupta called it a "document of moral decline" and the House unanimously referred the matter to the Public Accounts Committee for further investigation.

title: Billionaire founder Mark Cuban makes an AI prediction that employees may like
content: Mark Cuban predicts a future where AI adoption allows companies to shorten workdays by an hour, maintaining current pay. He urges businesses to embrace AI immediately, warning that those who don't will falter. Cuban emphasizes understanding AI's capabilities and risks, likening its memory to a savant but noting its lack of true understanding.

title: ‘Dhurandhar: The Revenge’ box office: Ranveer Singh's film inches close to Rs 1,000 crore
content: Despite a significant dip after its opening week, 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' has achieved a remarkable box office feat, nearing Rs 1000 crore globally within just seven days. The spy thriller, starring Ranveer Singh, experienced a massive opening weekend, breaking preview records before facing a decline in collections.

title: Microsoft EVP confirms ‘AI perk’ during hiring that Nvidia CEO proposed giving engineers
content: Job candidates are now negotiating AI token budgets alongside salaries, with some making it a condition of employment. This reflects the growing recognition of AI compute as a crucial resource for productivity, with tech leaders suggesting token budgets could be as high as half an engineer's salary.

title: A tale of two cities: Bengaluru or Hyderabad who wins the battle of boomtowns?
content: A new report highlights the intensifying rivalry between Bengaluru and Hyderabad, India's economic powerhouses. While Bengaluru leads in startup funding and tech roles, Hyderabad is rapidly closing the gap in office space and company formation. Both cities are crucial economic drivers, with spending patterns and commute times showing distinct differences.

title: India buys first LPG cargo from Iran in years, tanker was initially bound for China
content: The LPG shipment will be distributed among three state-run fuel retailers: Indian Oil Corporation, Bharat Petroleum Corporation Limited and Hindustan Petroleum Corporation Limited.

title: A’ja Wilson candidly recounts meeting with her fashion role model Beyoncé at Formula 1 race
content: A’ja Wilson reflected on meeting Beyoncé at a Formula 1 event, calling it a moment where “Nothing else matters.” The Las Vegas Aces star continues to grow her influence beyond basketball, blending fashion with elite performance. While embracing style and major platforms like the Met Gala, Wilson remains focused on her game, balancing off-court presence with her dominance and achievements on the court.

title: Candace Owens defends his Erika Kirk remarks; debate over free speech grows
content: Candace Owens supported Joe Rogan after his comments about Erika Kirk resurfaced online. She said Rogan speaks what people already think, which is why he stays popular. The issue started from an old video where Kirk discussed serious topics. Now, the debate has shifted to free speech, public opinion, and whether such comments go too far.

title: Philadelphia Eagles add $5M wide receiver to help Jalen Hurts elevate passing game
content: The Philadelphia Eagles have signed Elijah Moore to bolster their passing attack, aiming to provide Jalen Hurts with another reliable target. Moore's speed and ability to create separation are expected to ease pressure on top receivers and improve offensive rhythm. Despite a down year in 2025, his previous production and college connection with A.J.  if not, the pressure will remain on Jalen Hurts to elevate the offence. Either way, the Eagles have made it clear. They expect their passing game to improve in

title: 14 dead, 20 injured after private bus collides with lorry, bursts into flames in Andhra
content: A tragic accident occurred when a private travel bus collided with a tipper. The bus caught fire, resulting in the deaths of at least ten people. Around twenty-five passengers were on board. Twenty-one individuals sustained injuries and have been admitted to Markapur Hospital. Rescue efforts are ongoing as authorities investigate the cause of this devastating incident.

title: Virat Kohli sends IPL warning with 45 off 19 in RCB intra-squad game - Watch
content: Virat Kohli displayed explosive form with a 19-ball 45 in RCB's practice match, earning praise from mentor Dinesh Karthik. Kohli's consistent intent and drive to improve were highlighted. He was also seen bowling to struggling opener Phil Salt, showcasing team support. The IPL 2026 season opener is set for March 28.

title: 'Can’t tell him every single thing': Trump shown daily video of 'successful' strikes on Iran
content: The US military reported exceeding 10,000 strikes in its ongoing campaign, claiming it is ahead of schedule. However, a report suggests President Trump receives curated video updates highlighting only the most successful strikes, potentially omitting the full scope of the conflict. US Central Command stated the operation has significantly degraded Iran's naval and air defence capabilities

title: 'They'll be killed': Trump says Iran 'afraid' to admit it wants a deal
content: President Trump asserted Iran desires a deal but fears its own people and the US. He highlighted decisive military actions against Iran's nuclear ambitions, claiming US systems intercepted 100 missiles. Trump criticized past administrations for inaction and vowed to eliminate Iran's nuclear threat, comparing it to 'cutting out cancer'.

title: 'Thought of jumping from car': Sivaramakrishnan on how racism scars led to depression
content: Sivaramakrishnan, who played 25 international matches for India in a five-year career, said the struggles became severe during the IPL held in the UAE during the Covid-19 lockdown. “I was down completely and I didn’t want to see myself in the mirror. I would have a couple of drinks and go to sleep because I could not bear anything. Whenever I was awake, I thought I was going to die.”

title: Iran claims it shot down 'F-18' over Chabahar; US fact-check tells a different story
content: US Central Command has debunked Iranian claims of shooting down an American F-18 fighter jet near Chabahar.  CENTCOM explicitly stated on X that no US fighter aircraft have been downed by Iran, refuting footage shared by Iranian state media. This denial comes as Iran rejected a US ceasefire proposal, insisting on its own terms for ending hostilities.

title: Oil beyond $100: Crude climbs amid US-Iran tensions, fresh Hormuz curbs fears linger
content: Oil prices rebounded past $100 as markets reassess Middle East conflict developments. Iran's mixed signals on US peace proposals, coupled with Trump's tougher stance, create market uncertainty. Meanwhile, the ongoing conflict severely impacts global supply chains, particularly through the Strait of Hormuz, leading to significant oil disruptions.

title: Harish Rana finds his 'happy place' in tearless funeral
content: In a poignant farewell that ended a 12-year vigil, 31-year-old Harish Rana was cremated at the Green Park crematorium in Delhi. With tears held back, his younger brother Ashish ignited the pyre, supported by their sister, who embodied strength.

title: Businessman on cycle killed as car ‘driven by 17-year-old’ hits him near Mumbai
content: A 42-year-old businessman was killed on Kalyan's incomplete Ring Road after being hit by a speeding car allegedly driven by a 17-year-old boy. The boy fled the scene but was apprehended later. The victim's brother highlighted the victim's new cycling habit for fitness, while locals expressed concern over speeding and stunts on the unfinished road.

title: 'If they take any step forward ... ': Iran claims enemies preparing to occupy its island
content: Iran warns of potential island seizure by "enemy countries" with regional support, vowing "relentless" strikes on vital infrastructure. This comes amid a significant US military buildup in the Middle East, with analysts suggesting the UAE might be involved and Kharg Island as a potential target.

title: Prithvi Shaw seeks redemption: ‘Humans make mistakes but that’s history’
content: Returning to the IPL after a year's absence, Prithvi Shaw appears more focused and mentally stronger, having learned from past excesses. The break from cricket, coupled with personal reflection, has allowed him to rebuild his confidence and dedication, as he now prioritises his passion for the game and harbours hopes of an India comeback.

title: Op Urja Suraksha: Navy deploys 5 warships to guide cargo vessels exiting troubled Strait of Hormuz
content: India's Navy has launched Operation Urja Suraksha.  More than five frontline warships are ensuring safe passage for India-bound cargo ships.  These vessels carry vital supplies like LPG and crude oil.  The operation escorts ships out of the troubled Strait of Hormuz.  This protects critical maritime routes and Indian crew.  Key successes have already been achieved.

title: 'Don't repeat Rohit mistake': Ex-cricketer urges MI to stick with Hardik amid Surya captaincy noise
content: Mumbai Indians have several experienced players in the dressing room, including Rohit Sharma and Suryakumar Yadav, both of whom have led India in T20 cricket. A couple of seasons ago, the franchise replaced Rohit with Hardik as captain, ending a tenure that brought five IPL titles. The decision drew reactions from sections of fans, especially with Hardik moving from Gujarat Titans and taking charge immediately.

title: Are banks open or closed today on Ram Navmi? Chek state-wise list
content: Bank customers must check holiday schedules before visiting branches this week. Closures will occur due to festivals and state holidays.  RBI guidelines also mean branches will be shut on the second and fourth Saturdays of March.  Plan your banking needs accordingly. Digital platforms like mobile apps and UPI remain active for everyday transactions.  Services requiring branch visits will be unavailable.

title: GT playing XI for IPL 2026: Gill-Sudharsan to open, Holder vs Phillips in focus
content: Gujarat Titans enter IPL 2026 with continuity, retaining 20 players. After a third-place finish last season, they aim to refine their squad, adding five new faces including Jason Holder and Tom Banton. With a strong core featuring Sai Sudharsan and Rashid Khan, GT is poised for another playoff run.

title: 12 lakh Bengal voter claims rejected out of 32L vetted: EC official
content: Bengal's electoral rolls saw a significant reduction of approximately 12 lakh voters after judicial officers cleared just over half of the 60 lakh pending cases. This has led to widespread disenfranchisement in several rural pockets, with many long-term residents finding their names deleted despite submitting documentation.

title: Is Ram Navami on March 26 or 27? check correct date, time and significance
content: Ram Navami, celebrating Lord Shri Ram's birth, is observed with great fervor across India. This year, the festival falls on March 26, 2026, with Ayodhya and ISKCON devotees celebrating on March 27. The day marks the end of Chaitra Navratri and is observed with prayers, recitations of Ramacharitmanas, and acts of charity.

title: Stock market holiday on March 26: Will NSE, BSE remain shut for trading on Ram Navmi?
content: Investors can note that Dalal Street will observe a holiday on March 26 for Ram Navmi, as per the exchange's official calendar. This early pause in the trading week means equity markets will be closed. The article also lists upcoming stock market holidays for 2026, including Shri Mahavir Jayanti and Good Friday.

title: 'What's wrong?': David Warner loses cool at PSL captains’ presser - Watch
content: PSL 2026 faces multiple issues, including a fuel crisis and venue changes. During a captains’ press conference, David Warner showed frustration at others talking, saying, “What’s wrong? Sorry gentlemen, we have got school kids here.” PCB confirmed travel restrictions and cancelled events due to the crisis, with matches now limited to Lahore and Karachi.

title: UK court rejects Nirav Modi's bid to stall extradition
content: A UK court has dismissed fugitive diamantaire Nirav Modi's plea to reopen his extradition order, bringing him closer to facing charges in India for a Rs 6,500 crore Punjab National Bank scam.  The High Court found no exceptional circumstances to revisit the earlier decision, despite Modi's concerns about ill-treatment.  This development follows years of legal proceedings since his 2019 arrest.

title: Happy Ram Navami 2026: Best messages, quotes, wishes, and images to share
content: Ram Navami offers a unique, grounded morning, marked by quiet traditions and the enduring values of Lord Ram: balance, righteousness, and patience. Beyond rituals, the day emphasizes simple acts of connection, like sending wishes. The article provides heartfelt, thoughtful, and family-oriented messages, alongside concise lines for social media, encouraging a moment of reflection and peace amidst daily life.

title: Space co Agnikul successfully tests 3D-printed ‘Agnite’ booster engine
content: Indian space company Agnikul Cosmos has successfully tested its 3D-printed booster engine, 'Agnite'. This single-piece Inconel engine, a meter long, can be fully printed within a week, significantly reducing production complexity and turnaround time. The successful in-house testing marks a crucial end-to-end cycle completion for their larger engines.

title: 'Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone' teaser: Harry, Ron, Hermione return to Hogwarts
content: A fresh Harry Potter era dawns as HBO unveils a teaser for its 'Philosopher's Stone' series, introducing new actors like Dominic McLaughlin as Harry. The trailer revisits iconic moments from Harry's early life and his arrival at Hogwarts. This reimagining promises a more detailed exploration of J.K. Rowling's beloved saga, set to premiere this Christmas on HBO Max.

title: PM Modi remains world's most popular leader, says study by US firm
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has once again been recognized as the world's most popular democratic leader, achieving a 68% approval rating in a recent Morning Consult survey. This significant endorsement highlights his strong public support, even as it shows a slight decrease from last year's figures. Modi continues to lead global leaders by a considerable margin.
